subject: Add guard filter properties to controller
commit_message:
Add guard filter properties to controller

Adding new properties to guard Decision call.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2590
Change-Id: Ie4a37990e062c76ac4d6dd1b904ad354736fd27a
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
Signed-off-by: Pamela Dragosh <pdragosh@research.att.com>

Patch Set 3:

A brief description of the design of Step2 classes (I should put this in the comments for Step2):

- the step returns a list of whatever properties it needs, and UsecasesEventManager loadPreprocessorSteps() should have the smarts to determine what queries must be performed to provide the values
- there is a loadXxx() method associated with each of the properties that the step identified.  These can be overridden to do something different.  So if a step wants the whole VNF, the default method is sufficient.  However, if the step really just wants to extract pieces out of the VNF object, then it would override the appropriate loadXxxVnf() method and have it extract the relevant data from the VNF object
- Step2 setProperties() iterates through the properties and invokes the appropriate loadXxx() method.  If a step wants to load other properties that it didn't previously identify, then it can override this method and load the other properties (e.g., vserver id)
- one thing to note: the guard assumes the vserver id is always in the enrichment data.  If that's not the case, then it should include an additional property name indicating that the vserver id is needed.  Then UsecasesEventManager loadPreprocessorSteps() would have to be enhanced to obtain it via an aai query if it's not in the enrichment data.  Might even consider adding a GetEnrichmentVserverIdStep2, where the checks could be made

Patch Set 10:

> Patch Set 10:
> 
> There is progress here - but vCPE fails because it doesn't have vserver.vserver-name. Which is kind of making me scratch my head a bit.

Ok so A&AI actor is trying to do the custom query here, which would not be needed for vCPE. So how do we silently ignore it for filters? We can turn off guard for this use case and see if they want to support guard. If so, they would need to provide the vserver-name. I was thinking maybe we just return empty attributes in guard and let things go forward. There was another A&AI call to get vserver information based on vserver-id. But we would have to add that.

We can turn off guard for this use case and see if they want to support guard. If so, they would need to provide the vserver-name. I was thinking maybe we just return empty attributes in guard and let things go forward. There was another A&AI call to get vserver information based on vserver-id. But we would have to add that.

getPropertyNames could see if the vserver-name was provided and, if so, return the properties that require the CQ.  If it wasn't provided, then it could leave those properties out of the list.

Patch Set 11:

So what I need to figure out now, is for vCPE how do I ignore the missing vserver.vserver-name from the enrichment data. I also really can't see in the debug output where the guard call is happening, there is no logging. So I'll try to dig into that.

Patch Set 11:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> So what I need to figure out now, is for vCPE how do I ignore the missing vserver.vserver-name from the enrichment data. I also really can't see in the debug output where the guard call is happening, there is no logging. So I'll try to dig into that.

like I said, you could choose to leave the property names out of the list if there is no vserver-name in the enrichment data.  That would solve the vCPE case.

should be able to find "guard" or "/decision" in the output.  If you're using the frankfurt rules, then it's more difficult to line up, because all of the operations happen asynchronously, in parallel with the notifications generated by the rules.  OTOH, if you're using the usecases rules, then everything should line up because the rules control every step.

Patch Set 11:

FYI: the CQ sent back by the simulator can be found in policy-models.  Look for AaiCqResponse in the "simulators" project.

Patch Set 13:

Looks like you almost had it, just a couple of items had to be addressed:
- getEnrichment() is not optional - the data must exist or it throws an exception.  Since the vserver-name is supposed to be optional, I switched to event.getAai().get() instead
- the CQ data used by GuardStep2Test must be entirely constructed by hand (or I guess it would have been easier to load a json here - you can change it to do that if you're so inclined)

This DOES depend on the update to the simulator in policy-models.  With that, the junits ran in my eclipse.
